metadata.description: 持续集成 CI: Automatic build and test on every push. Jenkins/GitLab CI/GitHub Actions. For illustration purposes, here are some brief Python code examples for each of these continuous integration tools: ### Jenkins ```python # Example of a simple Jenkins pipeline script in Python (using the Jenkins Pipeline API) def jenkins_pipeline(): return """ pipeline { agent any stages { stage('Build') { steps { echo 'Building...' } } stage('Test') { steps { sh 'python3 -m unittest discover' } } } } """ ``` ### GitLab CI ```python # Example of a simple GitLab CI configuration in Python (using the GitLab API) def gitlab_ci(): return """ stages: - build - test build_job: stage: build script: - echo 'Building...' test_job: stage: test script: - python3 -m unittest discover """ ``` ### GitHub Actions ```python # Example of a simple GitHub Actions workflow in Python (using the GitHub API) def github_actions(): return """ name: Python application on: [push] jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v2 - name: Set up Python uses: actions/setup-python@v2 with: python-version: '3.x' - name: Install dependencies run: | python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip install -r requirements.txt - name: Run tests run: | python -m unittest discover """ ``` These examples provide a basic structure for each CI/CD tool, demonstrating how you might set up build and test stages.
